_README: Audited exception ledger for the reverse (contract ⊆ impl) conformance gate (issue #591). The sealed api.v1 declares every (path, method) below, but the shipped gateway+meeting-api pair does NOT serve it. gate:contract-conformance reads this file, reports each entry LOUDLY ('SEALED-BUT-WAIVED' / 'OWNED-ELSEWHERE'), and FAILS on any unimplemented sealed route that is NOT listed here. Adding a row is a deliberate, diff-visible act in the sealed contracts dir (the file is not a *.schema.json, so it does NOT change the api.v1 seal hash). Removing a gap means implementing the route (and deleting its row) or re-versioning the contract in a lane:contract PR. This is NOT a seal file and NOT a place to silence real drift cheaply — every row must carry a reason and an issue link.

owned_elsewhere._note: api.v1 paths under these prefixes are NOT the gateway+meeting-api REST pair's responsibility — the gateway forwards them to another service (admin-api, agent-api, the mcp service) or serves a gateway-local concern. They are declared by the sealed contract but conform under THAT service's surface, so the union-reverse check classifies them out of THIS gate's scope (reported as OWNED-ELSEWHERE, never silently dropped). Each prefix names its owner.

prefixes.note: gateway proxies /admin/{path} to admin-api under separate admin-key auth
chat/sessions surface; gateway exposes it as /agent/* (chat, sessions, reset)
Model-Context-Protocol endpoint; separate service, gateway proxies /mcp{,/path}
calendar connect/status/events/preferences; gateway exposes it under /user/calendar
per-user recording defaults; gateway exposes it under /user/*
workspace-git binding; user-config surface, not the meeting pair
GET / service index, not a data route

known_gaps.reason: No bot-command (send-to-meeting) backend in the 0.12 core — same class as /speak and /config, which also lack a downstream handler. Chat frames flow live over the WS va:*:chat channel; there is no REST send. Registering a 200 that does nothing would fake the capability, so the send half is left unimplemented and recorded here.
No on-demand re-transcribe orchestration exists in the 0.12 core (transcription is a live-pipeline concern). Left unimplemented + recorded rather than faked.

Bot-command channel (set avatar) — a live acts.v1 PUBLISH to an active bot, not meeting-api persistence. No downstream handler in the 0.12 carve (voice/media-agent carve, same family as /speak, /config).
Bot-command channel (clear avatar) — live acts.v1 PUBLISH, no downstream handler in the 0.12 carve (voice/media-agent carve).
Bot-command channel (start screen-share) — live acts.v1 PUBLISH, no downstream handler in the 0.12 carve (voice/media-agent carve).
Bot-command channel (stop screen-share) — live acts.v1 PUBLISH, no downstream handler in the 0.12 carve (voice/media-agent carve).

Bot-command channel (stop TTS) — live acts.v1 PUBLISH, no downstream handler in the 0.12 carve. The POST /speak counterpart IS forwarded by the gateway but likewise has no backend; the DELETE (stop) half is not even registered.

api.v1 declares it (desc: 'Forward to meeting-api GET /bots/{meeting_id}') but the gateway maps the meeting-detail read to GET /meetings/{meeting_id} instead; the dashboard reads /meetings/{id}. Deferred reconcile (drop from api.v1 or implement) is a lane:contract change (A1/A2, MATURITY-FINDINGS.md).

api.v1 declares recording delete; not implemented in the 0.12 carve (no consumer wired it — recording delete rides DELETE /meetings/{platform}/{native}, which purges the meeting's recordings). Deferred reconcile (A2, MATURITY-FINDINGS.md).

shape_gaps.reason: PATH restored by #666 (aliased to the moved POST /meetings/{platform}/{native}/share mint, so no more 404), but the RESPONSE SHAPE diverges: the 0.10 sealed TranscriptShareResponse is a public-URL share ({share_id, url, expires_at, expires_in_seconds}); the 0.12 mint returns a capability-token share ({id, token, mode, expires_at}). The public-URL-share backend is not in the 0.12 core. No golden example for this shape ships in golden/, so the golden-driven shape check does not enforce it today; recorded here as a restore-or-re-version decision, not a silent pass.
